title: Linear Regression, Explained Like You're Pricing Insurance for the First Time
description: A start-to-finish intuition-first explanation of Linear Regression: OLS, the cost function, the normal equation, and evaluation metrics—plus a closed-form implementation test.
author: Ujjwal Singh

title: Naive Bayes Explained: From Avocados to Spam Filters
description: Understand Naive Bayes intuitively using avocados and spam filtering.
author: Ujjwal Singh

title: Matplotlib for Machine Learning: A Complete Beginner's Guide
description: If you are starting your machine learning journey, you will hear one thing again and again: data alone does not tell a story. You have to see it to understand it. That is exactly why Matplotlib exists.
author: Ujjwal Singh

title: Linear Algebra for Machine Learning, Part 1: Vectors, Norms and the Foundations of Matrix Calculus
description: This is the first part in a linear algebra series covering vectors, norms, and the foundations of matrix calculus for ML.
author: Ujjwal Singh

title: NumPy for Machine Learning: A Complete Beginner's Guide
description: Before you can do anything in machine learning, whether that is loading a dataset, running matrix calculus, or feeding numbers into a model, you need a way to store and manipulate large collections of numbers efficiently.
author: Ujjwal Singh

title: Python Recall: The Concepts You Always Forget
description: This is not a complete Python course. It deliberately skips the basics you already know and skips OOP entirely, since that deserves its own separate note.
author: Ujjwal Singh

title: Understanding the Central Limit Theorem: From Coin Flips to One of the Most Beautiful Results in Statistics
description: The Central Limit Theorem (CLT) explained intuitively—starting from Bernoulli and Binomial distributions, building intuition for sampling distributions, and showing why sample means approach a Normal distribution.
author: Ujjwal Singh

title: Gradient Descent, Explained Like You're Actually Walking Down a Mountain
description: An intuition-first guide to Gradient Descent: cost surface, gradient, update rule, learning rate effects, and Batch/SGD/Mini-Batch with a from-scratch Python implementation.
author: Ujjwal Singh

title: Polynomial Regression: Fitting Curves With a Straight Line Model
description: Learn Polynomial Regression intuitively: how to expand features with PolynomialFeatures, why cross-terms like ab matter, and how to detect underfitting vs overfitting using learning curves.
author: Ujjwal Singh
